footer.signature: designed & built by yunus emre korkmaz

intro.marqueeText: • minimalism • ui ux design • perfectionism • aesthetics

welcome.name: [yunus emre korkmaz]
welcome.description: i design, and i write code. what i care about is making the complex simple — and the simple meaningful.

aboutHome.mainText: i don't rush. i leave every line and every empty space as if it will stay for years.

manifest.title: [thought]
manifest.desc: an interface is born not from pixels, but from decisions. i let go of the needless and leave only meaning behind.

aesthetic.label: aesthetics
aesthetic.text: beauty comes not from adding, but from taking away. let only what is needed remain.

entropy.label: entropy
entropy.text: order doesn't hold on its own; it takes effort. the moment you let go, it scatters.

rationality.label: rationality
rationality.text: to choose one path is to say goodbye to the others. to decide is to grow simpler.

contactHome.subtext: my door is open. if you have a good idea or a problem to solve, let's talk.

heroAbout.title: i think in design, and build with engineering.

aboutmePage.title: [who am i]
aboutmePage.bio_1: i'm yunus emre korkmaz. i'm both a front-end developer and a designer — but my real job is making people's lives easier.
aboutmePage.bio_2: i fuss over the small things: a space, a transition, a moment. because everything that looks easy actually takes a lot of effort.

whatIDo.title: [what i do]
whatIDo.desc_1: i design interfaces and write their code. i want them solid in structure and clear in intent.
whatIDo.desc_2: i don't care about showing off — i care about being understood. a thing working right comes before looking good.

discover.title: [discover me]
discover.subtitle: draw the shapes below; let them tell you how i work.

cards.title: the big picture
cards.description: i try to see the whole, not the pieces. user needs, business goals, and technical reality — i bring all three to the same table.

cards.title: design engineering
cards.description: i write code with a designer's eye. i turn an idea into pixels without losing its meaning.

cards.title: solid delivery
cards.description: i don't call it done until it feels right. i keep at it until it's fast, accessible, and reliable.

projects.header_desc: a selection of work i've designed and built over the past years. from municipal platforms to startup products, in all of them i was after the same thing: a simple interface and a flow that eases the user's job.

defaults.overview: simple, clear interfaces designed for real people to use.
defaults.conclusion: on this project what i wanted was a simple interface and a flow that eases the user's job. i worked to make it right first, then look good.

white.label: white
white.title: the weight of white
white.lead: malevich reset painting in 1918. i sit down in front of a white screen every day with the same question: what is worth carrying?

sections.heading: 1918: the zero point of painting
sections.body: in white on white, kazimir malevich threw away everything a painting could lose: the object, the color, the horizon, the story. what remained was a white square, slightly tilted, on a white ground. the two whites differ only in the texture of the brush and that small angle — the last two human marks left at the edge of absence.

sections.quote: i have broken the blue boundary of color limits, come out into the white; beside me comrade-pilots swim in this infinity.

sections.heading: white is not emptiness; it is light
sections.body: on a screen, white is not an absence the way it is on paper; it is the highest energy state, every pixel burning at full power. if i show you a white page, it is not because i am hiding something — it is because i am harnessing the light through architecture. those who mistake minimalism for giving up are wrong: the real work happens while deciding what stays.

sections.heading: what i want you to keep
sections.body: to simplify is not to impoverish. malevich's square taught me this: tear through the limit and what remains is not emptiness but infinity. remove the unnecessary from an interface, a sentence, a day; what is left is not less — it is clearer.

observer.label: observer
observer.title: to look is to touch
observer.lead: looking is not innocent. what is measured changes because it is measured.

sections.heading: a photon at the double slit
sections.body: the most unsettling lesson of quantum physics: a photon changes its behavior when you watch which slit it passes through. what acts like a wave becomes a particle under observation. measurement is not a glance from outside; it is a touch upon the system.

sections.heading: the user who knows they are watched
sections.body: the same law operates in interfaces. a user who feels watched does not behave naturally; every behavior turned into a metric bends. whatever i choose to measure, i have tilted the product in that direction. measurement is not neutral; it is a choice — and i try to make that choice on the user's behalf, not against them.

sections.heading: what i want you to keep
sections.body: assume an observer while writing code, while writing words, even while living. i write clean code because i know someone will read it; even the possibility of a gaze corrects behavior. you cannot erase the observer — but you can choose which observer you live by.

entropy.label: entropy
entropy.title: order is an expenditure
entropy.lead: order is not natural. order is effort that has been paid for.

sections.heading: the mind is an energy machine
sections.body: karl friston's free energy principle says something simple but ruthless: the mind spends energy ceaselessly to minimize surprise and uncertainty. anything unexpected is an error, and the mind works to close it. the systems i build obey the same law — staying stable demands constant energy.

sections.heading: a temporary war against chaos
sections.body: a flawless code architecture or a rational decision is a temporary front opened against the universe's natural chaos. entropy always returns; keeping an interface, an idea, or an order alive means continuously injecting energy against decay. the moment you cut the energy, the system reverts to its natural — that is, scattered — state.

sections.heading: is the cost worth paying?
sections.body: every order is a cost, but that cost is not meaningless. what i pay for is a clarity that reduces noise, sharpens the decision, and lightens the load on the user's mind. what we call aesthetics is often that cost made visible.

dichotomy.label: dichotomy
dichotomy.title: two voices, one signature
dichotomy.lead: there are two voices in my head: one makes the plan, the other burns it. my job is to keep them at the same table.

sections.heading: a beautiful lie
sections.body: the story that the left brain is rational and the right brain emotional is a neurological myth; the brain does not split that way. but the myth survives because the metaphor is true: two forces really do negotiate inside us. i use this lie knowingly — even a wrong map can describe the right territory.

sections.heading: the voice of order, the voice of chaos
sections.body: one draws boundaries, makes lists, cannot leave a thing unfinished. the other carries the silence of balıkesir, the anger of lost games, morning runs without a reason. for years i waited for one to defeat the other. then i realized: good work does not come from where the war ends — it comes from the front line.

sections.heading: what i want you to keep
sections.body: do not treat your inner contradiction as a bug to be fixed. the rational side gives the work its form, the emotional side gives it its reason. silence either one and you are left with a soulless machine or a scattered feeling. the signature is signed by both hands.

choice.label: choice
choice.title: the doors that close
choice.lead: to open one door is to turn the others into walls.

sections.heading: the dizziness of freedom
sections.body: kierkegaard defined anxiety as the dizziness of freedom. what you feel at the edge of a cliff is not the fear of falling but the knowledge that you could jump. everyone standing before five doors feels the same dizziness: what frightens is not the abundance of options — it is the irreversibility of choosing.

sections.heading: in architecture, every decision is a farewell
sections.body: choosing a framework, committing to a data model, saying no to a job — all of them are closing doors. in engineering we call this a trade-off, but the word is too sterile: in truth, you are saying goodbye to futures that will never be lived. before every big decision i pause; i wait for my finger to tremble. the tremble measures the weight of the decision.

sections.heading: what i want you to keep
sections.body: not choosing is also a choice — the most expensive one, because time closes every door. you can come back from the wrong door; you cannot come back from the one you never opened. choose despite the dizziness. it is enough that the light behind your door is worth the darkness of the other four.

loss.label: loss
loss.title: the gravity of loss
loss.lead: a gain and a loss of the same size do not pull equally. loss is always twice as heavy.

sections.heading: kahneman's scale
sections.body: kahneman and tversky measured it: the pain of a loss is roughly twice as intense as the joy of an equal gain. flip a coin: win 150, lose 100. the math says play; the gut says don't. that gut is not broken — it was simply calibrated for another age.

sections.heading: a gain is an event, a loss is a state
sections.body: the joy of a gain is a spike; you celebrate and return to your baseline. loss is different: the reference point itself moves down, and your old level hangs there as a ghost line you can no longer reach. the projects i deleted, the games i lost — they are all still hanging there. time does not erase that line; it only teaches you to look at it.

sections.heading: what i want you to keep
sections.body: the fear of loss decides quietly: we cling to sunk costs, stay in bad jobs, cannot delete dead code. i work by accepting the asymmetry — i design for reversibility, lose small, delete early. and i never let the user suffer an irreversible loss: the undo button is not a feature, it is an act of mercy.

dilemma.label: dilemma
dilemma.title: the mathematics of trust
dilemma.lead: two prisoners, two separate cells, one question: will you trust the other? game theory calls it a dilemma. i see the same question on the first day of every friendship, every bond.

sections.heading: 1950: two cells, one table
sections.body: the game was built at rand by merrill flood and melvin dresher; albert tucker gave it its story. two prisoners, two separate cells: talking looks like winning. since both sides know it, both talk — and both lose. mathematics calls this an equilibrium; the mind calls it a trap. and in a single hand it is even right — why open up to someone you will never see again?

sections.quote: the foundation of cooperation is not really trust, but the durability of the relationship.

sections.heading: the four-line winner
sections.body: in 1980 axelrod ran a tournament: which strategy wins the repeated dilemma? clever programs arrived from all over the world. the winner was anatol rapoport's four-line program: open with cooperation, then repeat the other's last move. nice, retaliatory, forgiving, readable. what won wasn't brilliance; it was consistency.

sections.heading: trusting first
sections.body: whoever begins open can be hurt. knowing this, i still open every first hand with trust, because nothing that begins closed ever weaves. if it isn't returned, i become a mirror — naivety is not a virtue. but i hold no grudge: the moment you come back, so do i. that is not forgetting; it is making the game possible again.

sections.heading: what the lines say
sections.body: life is not a single hand; we keep meeting the same people. every meeting either weaves or breaks, and years later what remains is not the decisions but the shape itself: who you wove with, who you broke from. i don't keep score — the shape is enough. trust is built not by words, but by repetition.

header.title: [manifest]
header.desc: i don't see design as decoration. i strip the unnecessary from an interface, a sentence, a day. what remains isn't less, it's clearer.

white_on_white.title: [white on white]
white_on_white.question: what does ‘white on white’ mean?
white_on_white.footer: in design, light is the father of form. hiding shadows is easy; mastering light requires craft. pure white is a screen's highest energy state. i do not hide the energy; i harness it through architecture.

dichotomy.title: [the dichotomy of mind]
dichotomy.desc: one mind, two lobes. both are me.
dichotomy.hint: click a side — and where they meet
dichotomy.left_desc: this side is my order. plans, boundaries, work i can't leave unfinished. a thing is either right or wrong; the in-between tires me. no ornament here. data in, decision out.
dichotomy.right_desc: this side is my chaos. the silence of balıkesir, the anger of lost games, morning runs without a reason. it won't follow a plan, it won't fit a measure. but everything that makes me, me, lives here.
dichotomy.center_desc: not pure logic, not pure feeling. human is where the two collide.

cost_of_order.title: [the entropy of mind]
cost_of_order.desc: order is not a state; it is an expense. it exists while you hold, and scatters when you let go. behind every standing system, someone is paying the cost. i pay it gladly.

observer.title: [the observer's tyranny]
observer.desc: the system holds all errors and all truths simultaneously until i observe it. reality is a heap of possibilities pruned by the attention of a rational observer. i don't write code; i force probabilities into a single rational output.
lines: i came into being the moment you looked.
look away, and i return to probability.
your attention is the lens that sharpens me.
without your gaze i am only noise.

choice.title: [the gravity of 'yes']
choice.desc: choosing is the art of subtraction. every yes i say costs a thousand ghosts. that is where the weight comes from: what i carry is not the decision, but everything i gave up for it.
choice.stage: five doors; you may open only one.
choice.final: the other four you will never read.

doors.full: maybe in another city, at another desk, you were asking the same question.
a line never written, a project never opened, a team you never met.

doors.whisper: that job you didn't say yes to...
doors.full: that job you didn't say yes to would have turned you into someone else; who, you'll never know.
a quieter life: less screen, more morning. you could have loved that one too.
someone like a mirror, who made every choice you didn't. you will never meet them.

asymmetry.title: [the asymmetry of loss]
asymmetry.desc: losing is not the negative of gaining; it pulls twice the weight. kahneman measured it, i live it at every delete key. if i am to tear down what i built, i wait for my finger to tremble.

dilemma.title: [the prisoner's dilemma]
dilemma.desc: trust is not a feeling; it is a strategy. in a single hand, betrayal wins — but i don't play single hands with anyone. if we're sitting at the same table again, the math changes.
dilemma.strategy: my rule is simple: i trust first. i do what you do. i hold no grudge.
dilemma.verdict_coop: five hands of trust. two lines braided into each other — this is what trust looks like.
dilemma.verdict_mixed: it broke here and there, yet it wove. what matters is not never breaking, but coming back.
dilemma.verdict_betray: i did what you did. what remains is not a braid — just two lonely lines, side by side.
dilemma.hint: trust or betray — both lines are drawn at once

contactPage.sidebar_title: please contact me to set up an online meeting or ask any questions you have.

successBox.title: ● message sent
successBox.text: thank you — i’ll get back to you as soon as possible.
